description_long: This object appears to be a control panel or dashboard, featuring a variety of analog dials, switches, and labeled sections. The left side is dominated by a series of round gauges and meters, likely for monitoring different parameters, while the right side contains more switches and possibly indicator lights. The surface is mostly flat with a slight protrusion at the bottom center, possibly for mounting or cable management. The color scheme is primarily light gray and beige, with some blue and black accents for labels and controls. The materials suggest a combination of metal and plastic, typical for durable electronic control equipment. This type of panel would be found in technical or industrial urban environments, such as utility rooms, garages, or maintenance areas.
description: A rectangular control panel with multiple analog dials, switches, and labeled sections in light gray and beige, likely made of metal and plastic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full array of dials, switches, and labels, with the most detail visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and the arrangement of controls along the surface.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a mostly plain surface with minimal detail, some labels or ports visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and the edge of the control surface.
